an1.333:1.1: "Just as, *bhikkhus*, the delightful gardens, delightful forests, delightful grounds, and delightful lotus ponds in this Jambudīpa are few;
an1.333:1.2: yet far more numerous are these: the rugged and uneven banks, the treacherous river crossings, the places of stumps and thorns, and the rough mountains;
an1.333:1.3: so too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings that are land-born; far more numerous are those beings that are water-born.
an1.334:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who are reborn among humans;
an1.334:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who are reborn elsewhere than among humans.
an1.335:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who are reborn in the middle countries;
an1.335:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who are reborn in the border countries, among those without understanding, among barbarians.
an1.336:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who possess discernment [*paññā*], are not dull, not mentally deficient, and are capable of understanding the meaning of what is well-spoken and ill-spoken;
an1.336:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who are of little discernment [*paññā*], dull, mentally deficient, and incapable of understanding the meaning of what is well-spoken and ill-spoken.
an1.337:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings endowed with the noble eye of discernment [*paññā*];
an1.337:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who have gone into blindness [*avijjā*], bewildered.
an1.338:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who get to see the Tathāgata;
an1.338:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who do not get to see the Tathāgata.
an1.339:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who get to hear the *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayaṁ*] proclaimed by the Tathāgata;
an1.339:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who do not get to hear the *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayaṁ*] proclaimed by the Tathāgata.
an1.340:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having heard the *dhamma*, retain it;
an1.340:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, having heard the *dhamma*, do not retain it.
an1.341:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who examine the meaning of *dhammas* that have been retained;
an1.341:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who do not examine the meaning of *dhammas* that have been retained.
an1.342:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having understood the meaning and the *dhamma*, practice in accordance with the *dhamma*;
an1.342:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, having understood the meaning and the *dhamma*, do not practice in accordance with the *dhamma*.
an1.343:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who are stirred at occasions that inspire stirring;
an1.343:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who are not stirred at occasions that inspire stirring.
an1.344:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, being stirred, exert themselves wisely;
an1.344:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, being stirred, do not exert themselves wisely.
an1.345:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, making relinquishment their object, attain *samādhi*, attain singleness of mind [*cittassa ekaggatā*];
an1.345:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, making relinquishment their object, do not attain *samādhi*, do not attain singleness of mind [*cittassa ekaggatā*].
an1.346:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who receive the finest food and the finest flavors;
an1.346:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who do not receive the finest food and the finest flavors, subsisting by gleaning, living on what is brought in a bowl.
an1.347:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who receive the taste of meaning, the taste of *dhamma*, the taste of liberation;
an1.347:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who do not receive the taste of meaning, the taste of *dhamma*, the taste of liberation.
an1.347:1.3: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, you should train yourselves thus:
an1.347:1.4: 'We will be those who receive the taste of meaning, the taste of *dhamma*, the taste of liberation.'
an1.347:1.5: This is how you should train yourselves, *bhikkhus*."
an1.348-350:1.1: "Just as, *bhikkhus*, the delightful gardens, delightful forests, delightful grounds, and delightful lotus ponds in this Jambudīpa are few;
an1.348-350:1.2: yet far more numerous are these: the rugged and uneven banks, the treacherous river crossings, the places of stumps and thorns, and the rough mountains.
an1.348-350:1.3: So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having passed away from among humans, are reborn among humans; far more numerous are those beings who, having passed away from among humans, are reborn in hell … are reborn in the animal realm … are reborn in the realm of ghosts."
an1.351-353:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having passed away from among humans, are reborn among the gods;
an1.351-353:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, having passed away from among humans, are reborn in hell … are reborn in the animal realm … are reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.354-356:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having passed away from among the gods, are reborn among the gods;
an1.354-356:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, having passed away from among the gods, are reborn in hell … are reborn in the animal realm … are reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.357-359:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having passed away from among the gods, are reborn among humans;
an1.357-359:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, having passed away from among the gods, are reborn in hell … are reborn in the animal realm … are reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.360-362:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having passed away from hell, are reborn among humans;
an1.360-362:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, having passed away from hell, are reborn in hell … are reborn in the animal realm … are reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.363-365:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having passed away from hell, are reborn among the gods;
an1.363-365:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, having passed away from hell, are reborn in hell … are reborn in the animal realm … are reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.366-368:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having passed away from the animal realm, are reborn among humans;
an1.366-368:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, having passed away from the animal realm, are reborn in hell … are reborn in the animal realm … are reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.369-371:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having passed away from the animal realm, are reborn among the gods;
an1.369-371:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, having passed away from the animal realm, are reborn in hell … are reborn in the animal realm … are reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.372-374:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having passed away from the realm of ghosts, are reborn among humans;
an1.372-374:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, having passed away from the realm of ghosts, are reborn in hell … are reborn in the animal realm … are reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.375-377:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are those beings who, having passed away from the realm of ghosts, are reborn among the gods;
an1.375-377:1.2: far more numerous are those beings who, having passed away from the realm of ghosts, are reborn in hell … are reborn in the animal realm … are reborn in the realm of ghosts."
